market_context: US stocks edged higher on Monday with the S&P 500 up 0.4% near 6620, testing resistance amid seasonal tailwinds and broad sector strength despite Iran tensions and a Trump deadline for resolution. Geopolitical risks from Middle East conflicts drove March declines (S&P 500 -5%) and oil volatility, but analysts maintain a bull market thesis with resilient economy, shrinking deficit, and expected Fed rate cuts later in 2026. Sentiment is neutral (put-call ratio 0.97), with upside potential to 6900 if 6620 breaks.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 closed Monday at ~6620, up 0.4%, testing key resistance with head-and-shoulders pattern signaling upside to 6900 if broken; seasonal April strength and neutral sentiment (put-call 0.97) support continuation.[1] Broader market internals show strength in select sectors, outweighing Iran wildcard risks.[1][2]

Nasdaq followed S&P/Dow gains Monday amid market broadening beyond tech into industrials and consumer staples; bull thesis intact with strong fundamentals despite March's 5% pullback from Middle East risks.[2][4] Expected Fed rate cuts later 2026 to aid growth stocks.[2]

Dow gained alongside S&P Monday, with early positive response to shrinking FY2026 deficit ($697B, -17% YoY) favoring consumer/industrial stocks; resilient US energy buffers oil shocks.[1][2] Healthy market broadening supports industrials uptrend.[2]
March saw energy sector red amid oil spikes from Middle East supply shocks, but potential Iran resolution by 8pm ET deadline could ease pressures; profit-taking in energy after volatility.[1][3]
